title: Minimum Operations to Make Array Equal II
titleCn: 使数组中所有元素相等的最小操作数 II

testcases.input: nums1 = [4,3,1,4], nums2 = [1,3,7,1], k = 3 Output: 2 Explanation: In 2 operations, we can transform nums1 to nums2. 1st operation: i = 2, j = 0. After applying the operation, nums1 = [1,3,4,4]. 2nd operation: i = 2, j = 3. After applying the operation, nums1 = [1,3,7,1]. One can prove that it is impossible to make arrays equal in fewer operations.
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: In 2 operations, we can transform nums1 to nums2. 1st operation: i = 2, j = 0. After applying the operation, nums1 = [1,3,4,4]. 2nd operation: i = 2, j = 3. After applying the operation, nums1 = [1,3,7,1]. One can prove that it is impossible to make arrays equal in fewer operations.

testcases.input: nums1 = [3,8,5,2], nums2 = [2,4,1,6], k = 1 Output: -1 Explanation: It can be proved that it is impossible to make the two arrays equal.
testcases.expectedOutput: -1 Explanation: It can be proved that it is impossible to make the two arrays equal.
